feed.title: RA: Resident Advisor Magazine
feed.subtitle: News, features, reviews and podcasts from RA

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Om Unit
entry.content: Tracing a forgotten thread between street soul and the hardcore continuum.

entry.title: Seth Troxler's Slacker 85 label to release 35-track VA compilation
entry.content: He Ain't Here features Troxler, Skream, Hiroko Yamamura and many more.

entry.title: StubHub UK fined £900k over hidden ticket fees, ordered to refund 50,000 customers
entry.content: The competition regulator said the firm breached consumer law by adding mandatory charges at checkout rather than displaying full ticket prices upfront.

entry.title: Traxsource launches AI transparency labels
entry.content: The download store is the latest to release classification systems for AI-generated and assisted music.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: d.jezzz
entry.content: Laidback beats for a sunset wind-down.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Dexter Colt & Sunday Lee
entry.content: Deep dance cuts for mind, body and soul.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Gizmoe
entry.content: A genre-traversing session that reaffirms the dance floor's status as a place of protest.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Malibu & Crystallmess
entry.content: Suspended between dream and dissociation.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Icarus Redux
entry.content: Politically-charged soundscapes with Djrum-inspired scratching.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: HeavenandHelen
entry.content: Buttery smooth Afro tech and deep house.

entry.title: Montreal club Salon Daomé marks 25 years this Friday
entry.content: The Plateau-Mont-Royal institution is celebrating with an extended-hour event with Derrick Carter this Friday.

entry.title: AIAIAI launches white versions of its TMA-2 and Tracks headphones
entry.content: The new colourway will debut as the exclusive headphones for Björk's touring exhibition, Echolalia.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: pink.wav & Andreas Palmer
entry.content: Dubwise delights from the beloved Athenian DJs.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: OG Militant B
entry.content: A head-scrambling mix from the Tokyo wax specialist.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: DJ Marfox
entry.content: Rhythmic mastery, live from Ormside Projects.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Que DJ
entry.content: An hour-long gqom masterclass.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Felix Dickinson
entry.content: Groove-led selections, recorded on the terrace of Colombia's El Rio Hostel.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: A Lizard Called A
entry.content: A Munich mainstay in session.

entry.title: New hi-fi listening space Tigris opens in Detroit
entry.content: The Mexicantown space features a cafe, bar and sound room.

entry.title: Fundraiser launched for Auckland venue Neck of the Woods
entry.content: $109,000 NZD has been raised in an attempt to keep the Karangahape Road space in operation.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Zokhuma
entry.content: Shimmery, slippery techno from a Goa beach party.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: oma totem
entry.content: An absorbing live set from the multifaceted Catalan artist.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: EVEHIVE
entry.content: Baile funk, techno and club hybrids recorded live from Carnaval.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Bibi
entry.content: Effortlessly engrossing selections.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Patrick Russell
entry.content: One of New York's sharpest selectors outdoes himself.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Azzi On The Beat & WearAreAllChemicals
entry.content: Get to know Mara, the Nigerian subgenre of Afrobeats.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Ciel
entry.content: Chillout music with an aqueous feel.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Larry Heard
entry.content: An exclusive recording of the master in action from 2005.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Pépe
entry.content: A masterclass in eclecticism.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Appleblim
entry.content: A dubstep architect showcases his new album.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Richard Chater
entry.content: One of Glasgow's local heroes in session.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: DJ Earl Grey
entry.content: Laidback grooves to ease into the week.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Sarayu
entry.content: Groovy tech-house heaters .

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Der Opium Queen
entry.content: Dense downtempo and oddball house.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Sisi
entry.content: Remembering a legend of Japanese dance music.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Moonshine
entry.content: A global club music showcase direct from a Zanzibari beach.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Diogo Lacerda & Godero
entry.content: Deep cuts on one of Lisbon's best sound systems.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Icehead
entry.content: Breaks, acid and house.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Hyas & Tactic
entry.content: Divine house and jazzy broken beat.

entry.title: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani appears at Nowadays, Paragon
entry.content: Mamdani dropped into a handful of clubs on behalf of Congressional candidate Claire Valdez.

entry.title: Legendary music mogul Clive Davis dies at 94
entry.content: The music industry powerhouse birthed countless careers across a six-decade career.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Plus One
entry.content: One of Dublin's finest.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Gwenan
entry.content: High-level improvisation.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Gabrielle Kwarteng
entry.content: The newest STUDIO resident at New York club BASEMENT.

entry.title: Voices Radio to open new East London studio
entry.content: Voices East will focus on "DJs and video-first" content.

entry.title: New listening bar and cultural space to open in Belfast
entry.content: Music Room launches on July 1st.

entry.title: Amsterdam club TILLATEC announces closure
entry.content: "This is not just a goodbye to a building, it's a love letter to the people who made it mean something," the founders said in a statement.

entry.title: RA.1044 Love Injection
entry.content: A lesson in universal love, told via deep club grooves.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Bundang Rotary Cicadas
entry.content: Two hours of laidback house and downtempo.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: DJ Scott
entry.content: Rapid-fire makina from 2004.

entry.title: Playlist: Horst 2026
entry.content: Listen to 65 tracks that the RA team heard on the ground at this year's festival.

entry.title: Playlist: Draaimolen 2025
entry.content: Listen to 66 tracks heard by RA staff at the Dutch festival last weekend.

entry.title: Playlist: Houghton 2025
entry.content: Listen to 110 tracks RA staff heard on the ground at the UK festival this year.

entry.title: Playlist: Horst Arts & Music 2025
entry.content: Listen to 85 tracks that defined this year's edition of the Brussels festival.

entry.title: Reptant - Ballet Robotique
entry.content: Ice-cold 808s from electro's new prince.

entry.title: Boards of Canada - Tape 05
entry.content: Warp's mysterious duo are back after 13 years. The surprise: it's some of their most beautiful work yet.

entry.title: DJ International Records hosts Juneteenth takeover at Avalon Regal Theater
entry.content: The event features Joe Smooth, Max-A-Million, Chip E. and more.

entry.title: fakemink - Terrified
entry.content: After crossing over on a wave of hype, the self-proclaimed saviour of the UK underground wastes his moment on a bloated album of trap, self-aggrandisement and poorly mixed electronics.

entry.title: Boards of Canada - Inferno
entry.content: Warp's IDM icons resurface after 13 years with a dark, disquieting record shaped by a violent world. Say goodbye to the fuzz and wistfulness.

entry.title: RHR - GÍRIA
entry.content: Thrilling baile funk fusions from one of São Paulo's most compelling club whisperers.

entry.title: Zora Jones - ANGEL CRISIS
entry.content: Zora Jones's first solo project in five years ushers in a new era with transformative, high-drama bass music.

entry.title: Excalibur and DJ Spence - Medium Rare
entry.content: Fatigued by the fast pace of algorithmic slop? Reach for this EP of unhurried perfection from one of minimal's best labels.

entry.title: Mala & Magugu - Militant Don
entry.content: Mala goes back to basics with heavyweight results.

entry.title: Beatrice M. - Midnight Swim
entry.content: Another hit from France's best hope for a modern day dubstep great.

entry.title: Jasmín - Bite The Hand That Feeds You
entry.content: Hessle Audio's 50th release reflects on the label's techno-dubstep roots while barrelling bass-first into the future.

entry.title: This week's new music: BAE BAE, Nikki Nair, Ma Sha
entry.content: We round up ten new music announcements from the last few days.

entry.title: Vancouver's Public Disco announces ten-year anniversary festival
entry.content: The one-day October event will feature Leon Vynehall, Fred P, Prosumer and others.

entry.title: New club Dr. Banana opens in São Paulo
entry.content: Located in the Vergueiro neighborhood, the venue is inspired by the golden age of the city's club life.

entry.title: Headset finalises 2026 Pride programming
entry.content: The Scottish promoter will host three parties in Glasgow, Edinburgh and Bristol.

entry.title: Answer Code Request - Halo
entry.content: The Berlin producer picks up where his 2018 LP Gens left off, wringing extreme feeling from techno's austere templates.

entry.title: Thaiboy Digital & swedm® - Paradise
entry.content: The Drain Gang mainstay's latest LP takes on big-room trance with aplomb.

entry.title: james K - Friend Remixes
entry.content: One of last year's best albums is recast into a set of aqueous club jams, anchored throughout by the precision of james K's voice.

entry.title: A new tool allows artists to check whether their tracks are used in AI
entry.content: AI Watchdog stems from an investigation from The Atlantic into AI data training.

entry.title: Australia's Soma Festival reveals 2026 lineup
entry.content: HTRK, Piezo, Reptant and Simo Cell are among the artists heading to New South Wales this November.

entry.title: Oxfam launches collaborative protest track featuring artists from Palestine, DRC and South Sudan
entry.content: "No More Radio Silence" arrives as part of the charity's campaign spotlighting underreported global conflicts and Refugee Week's On Air programme.

entry.title: This week's top stories: RIP Olympios, Houghton lineup, Rawhide opens in New York
entry.content: Five key electronic music stories from the last few days.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: jayah
entry.content: A slow-burn of deep grooves from a Chicago community staple.

entry.title: Loukeman - SD-3
entry.content: A restless collage of folk, pop, R&B and rap, stitched into emotionally charged electronics.

entry.title: Jump Source - Fold
entry.content: Priori and Patrick Holland have been on a tear, but their debut LP as Jump Source hits harder. It's one of the sharpest, best-built electronic albums you'll hear this year.

entry.title: RA.1041 Chlär
entry.content: Undeniably funky techno.

entry.title: RA.1040 Clementaum
entry.content: The São Paulo-based artist tears through baile funk, ballroom and techno in a turbo-charged showcase of global club music's cutting edge.

entry.title: RA.1039 K Wata
entry.content: Sleek, sensual bass science that sketches a new blueprint for dub in the 2020s.

entry.title: RA.1038 The Trip
entry.content: 90 minutes of blissful, sun-soaked house from the essential UK producer duo.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Cascou
entry.content: Acid folk, warm Balearic and soft-hued jazz-funk.

entry.title: Atlanta venue Palo Santo to close this month
entry.content: The closure comes as the team behind the restaurant and rooftop lounge launches two new hospitality concepts.

entry.title: QNCC to host town hall with New York government
entry.content: The meeting will discuss the future of nightlife in the city with leaders from the Mamdani administration.

entry.title: Rosa Pistola announces compilation celebrating history and artists of Mexican tribal
entry.content: Dioses Tribales is out in July via Trucha Soul, Sabotaje Media and La Chekera Records.

entry.title: Chicago's Podlasie Club unveils lineup for Juneteenth celebrations
entry.content: DJ THADZ, DJ Heather, Byrell The Great and Floor Supervisor will take part in SUGARWATER this weekend.

entry.title: 2 Live Crew's Uncle Luke to run for Congress
entry.content: The Miami rapper will vie for a US House seat in a South Florida primary this August.

entry.title: Mount Kimbie to release fabric presents mix
entry.content: Out July 31st, the compilation includes two new tracks from the duo.

entry.title: Auckland venue Neck of the Woods to close after 11 years
entry.content: The Karangahape Road space said rising debt, declining bar revenue and ongoing post-pandemic pressures have forced it to shut its doors next week.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Staryu
entry.content: A seamless survey of Thai grooves.

entry.title: RA to host Reddit AMA with Tiga
entry.content: The Turbo Recordings founder will answer questions on r/TheOverload later today.

entry.title: Fimiguerrero unveils surprise EP with Bassvictim, fakemink
entry.content: The Statue Of A Fool is out now.

entry.title: Houghton's 2026 lineup has appeared online
entry.content: The UK festival will welcome more than 60 debuts this summer.

entry.title: RA and Dancefloor Intimacy to host accessible nightlife workshop
entry.content: The online session will take place next Wednesday.

entry.title: DJ Heartstring announce three-date Australian tour
entry.content: The Berlin duo will play Naarm, Eora and Boorloo this September.

entry.title: London's Body Movements Festival finalises 2026 lineup
entry.content: The Southwark Park one-dayer has added Horse Meat Disco and House Gospel Choir.

entry.title: Saul Williams reveals new album with Carlos Niño, Moor Mother, Kamasi Washington
entry.content: Leap Life also features Georgia Anne Muldrow, Suryva Botofasina and Massive Attack's 3D.

entry.title: New York's MoMA PS1 unveils Warm-Up lineup
entry.content: Mark Ernestus, Lloyd Barnes, Marcellus Pittman and keiyaA are set to perform at the outdoor concert series.

entry.title: Zora Jones unveils first album in six years
entry.content: 50 Bullets To The Heart lands later this year.

entry.title: Two Shell's new album taps into the the blurring of human and machine
entry.content: Infinite Now is out on October 16th.

entry.title: DJ Spanish Fly announces new album, Cadillac Warning
entry.content: The ten-track LP features recordings spanning the 1990s from the Memphis rap pioneer.

entry.title: Stockholm club Spice 99 launches open-air listening bar
entry.content: Spice H2O is a seasonal outdoor space on the Årstaviken waterfront.

entry.title: 'We're not giving up': Berlin club Cassiopeia faces closure after eviction notice
entry.content: The Friedrichshain venue said it has two weeks to vacate the RAW site after owner Kurth Group withdrew from planning negotiations.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Givi Gelashvili
entry.content: Deep cuts from a Tbilisi veteran.

entry.title: Rimarkable launches new label, Betty Jean's Dream
entry.content: The inaugural release, by the Detroit artist herself, arrives this Friday, June 19th.

entry.title: Detroit venues Spot Lite and UFO Bar to close
entry.content: The clubs will shut at the end of this month.

entry.title: New York's Pioneer Works announces open call for residency
entry.content: The programme offers visual artists and musicians studio space and institutional resources.

entry.title: Film: The Sound of the 2020s Is…Nothing?
entry.content: No new sound has fully broken through in the 2020s. We explore why.

entry.title: Beverly Glenn-Copeland releases newly unearthed song
entry.content: "Light Of The Heavens" was originally recorded after his 1986 album Keyboard Fantasies.

entry.title: Antwerp club Ampere to close its doors after losing concession
entry.content: The space, located underneath the city's train station, will instead be retooled as a community venue.

entry.title: New evilgiane album features Nourished by Time, Cash Cobain, xaviersobased
entry.content: #HEAVENSGATE Vol.2 is out June 19th.

entry.title: London club The Cause launches new festival focused on independent radio communities
entry.content: The Cause FM: New Frequency will include takeovers from Refuge Worldwide and Seoul Community Radio on July 25th.

entry.title: Brussels venue Cabaret Mademoiselle to close
entry.content: The burlesque space will permanently shut its doors on July 20th.

entry.title: Techno DJ Olympios killed in alleged double homicide in western Greece
entry.content: The Renegade Methodz affiliate died alongside his mother. A 65-year-old man has been charged with their murders.

entry.title: Berlin event series Büro Siebzig returns to Globus for nine-week summer live series
entry.content: The weekly concert programme features British Murder Boys, Shackleton, Milan W. and others.

entry.title: Top Ten Festivals 2026: July
entry.content: Each month, we highlight ten of the best festivals happening around the world.

entry.title: Free therapy programme for artists launches in India
entry.content: Compos Mentis is "India's first community-funded mental health initiative specifically for musicians," according to the organisers.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Lazarusman
entry.content: The many shades of house, from gospel to Afro house and more.

entry.title: Deezer launches AI music detector for all streaming platforms
entry.content: The tool will allow users to check if their playlists include AI-generated tracks.

entry.title: Carl Cox, Judge Jules recognised in King's Birthday Honours
entry.content: The UK dance music veterans received an OBE and MBE respectively.

entry.title: New venue Club Rawhide opens in New York
entry.content: The Carry Nation, DJ Miss Parker and Junior M were among the Chelsea club's first bookings.

entry.title: Listen back to six music industry talks with Rough Trade, Bandcamp, YouTube
entry.content: The Rising Artist Foundation and Honcho have published panel discussions from their 2025 incubator programme, Fourth River.

entry.title: Ricardo Villalobos, Skee Mask, Saoirse, Yaleesa Hall remix Galcher Lustwerk
entry.content: The Vestibule Remixes EP is out on July 3rd.

entry.title: Join RA in the charity ADE Cycle 2026
entry.content: The tenth anniversary of the annual fundraiser will see artists and industry professionals ride from London to Amsterdam in support of music education programmes in South Africa.

entry.title: Oliver Tree, US artist and former R&S Records signee, dies aged 32
entry.content: The California musician was killed in a helicopter crash in Brazil yesterday.

entry.title: Galician festival MOURA reveals 2026 lineup
entry.content: mad miran, oma totem and Sugar Free are among the artists heading to Ourense this September.

entry.title: New documentary charts the rise of North East venue Are You Affiliated
entry.content: Ryan Lilburn's documentary explores how a working men's club in North Shields became a hub for music and community activity.

entry.title: RA.1043 Pretty Girl
entry.content: 90 minutes of wistful house from the talented Steel City Dance Discs affiliate, perfect for easing into the night or winding down after the party.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: OAARIKI
entry.content: Thrilling selections from all-Aotearoa artists.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Doc Scott & MC Justyce
entry.content: Archival drum & bass, recorded live at The Blue Note in 1997.

entry.title: This week's new music: DJ Koze, Eden Samara, FOREIGNER
entry.content: We round up ten new music announcements from the last few days.

entry.title: Leticia van Riel, Sama' Abdulhadi's co-manager, dies following car accident
entry.content: IMS paid tribute to "a force within the electronic music industry"

entry.title: horsegiirL - Nature Is Healing
entry.content: The debut album from the world's first half-horse, half-human DJ is a unique accomplishment for both species.

entry.title: Arc'teryx Academy Music Series returns to Chamonix
entry.content: The three-night music programme will feature Theo Parrish, Shanti Celeste and more.

entry.title: Tokyo's NU Festival reveals lineup for inaugural edition
entry.content: Actress, Nathan Fake and Sapphire Slows have been booked for the three-day event later this month.

entry.title: This week's top stories: RIP Trevor Dietz, final Soft Cell album, Shlømo criminal complaint
entry.content: Five key electronic music stories from the last few days.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: DJ Caio Prince
entry.content: A set of funk bruxaria that goes the distance.

entry.title: Toronto festival SOJOURN shares 2026 lineup
entry.content: Ellen Allien, TDJ, Ahadadream, Neffa-T and more will play the September event.

entry.title: New course teaches production concepts with cat illustrations
entry.content: The course targets producers who may struggle with foundational concepts.

entry.title: L'Rain announces new album fata morgana
entry.content: The Brooklyn artist's fourth LP arrives via Mexican Summer on August 14th.

entry.title: DJ Steven, cofounder of Metropolis Music and Bulgarian scene architect, dies aged 64
entry.content: "We can't find the words to describe the emptiness in our hearts," the promoter shared.

entry.title: London festival Margins United unveils 2026 lineup
entry.content: The September celebration of East and South-East Asian culture has booked DJ Love, jackzebra and tamanaramen.

entry.title: Donna Summer estate signs rights partnership with Primary Wave
entry.content: The agreement covers the late disco icon's catalogue, recordings and name, image and likeness rights.

entry.title: Prague festival Lunchmeat unveils first wave of artists for 2026
entry.content: Autechre, umru, upsammy & Valentina Magaletti and more will play the Prague festival this September.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Sewa Choki
entry.content: A comprehensive look at the Austin-based up-and-comer.

entry.title: Mexico City club M.N.Roy unveils World Cup programming
entry.content: Acts such as Omar Latigo, Theus Mago and Musumeci will play from June 11th through 27th.

entry.title: Scott Anderson, public records cofounder, dies
entry.content: "He had this supernatural ability to bring light into any situation," the venue shared.

entry.title: Milan-based platform VENA unveils new festival
entry.content: FLYSCH Festival takes place in the Marche region on July 30th to August 2nd.

entry.title: Lyra Pramuk changes name to Alif Hilal following conversion to Islam
entry.content: The Berlin-based vocalist, producer and composer said the change reflects her spiritual path and artistic alignment.

entry.title: Warner Music Group acquires AI protection tool startup Sureel
entry.content: The platform promises to protect music and other IP from unauthorised use by AI training models.

entry.title: Bonobo reveals new album Distance In Static
entry.content: The Ninja Tune LP, featuring Joy Crookes, Arooj Aftab and Nilüfer Yanya, lands in September.

entry.title: New club Bastet opens in Philadelphia
entry.content: The venue takes over the former Palmer Social Club building.

entry.title: Chicago collective TRENCH releases VA compilation
entry.content: The 42-track release features Traxman, DJ Manny, DJ Travella, DJ Thadz and more.

entry.title: Sarah Davachi announces new album The Will of Tongues
entry.content: A single is out now through her Late Music label.

entry.title: Criminal complaint filed against Shlømo
entry.content: METOODJS and lawyer Maître Inès Davau said a complaint was lodged before the Paris Judicial Court last Friday.

entry.title: New festival Solus launches in Glasgow
entry.content: The one-day event will take place under the bridge at West Street Subway Station on August 15th.

entry.title: Nyege Nyege announces first European festival in Brussels
entry.content: MIRROR WORLD will take place on September 25th and 26th.

entry.title: Trevor Dietz, Fontaines D.C. manager and Dublin scene innovator, dies aged 47
entry.content: "He cared passionately for us and for what was fair and right in the wider world," the band shared.

entry.title: Berlin inclusion project ICK MACH WELLE launches fundraiser, compilation amid funding crisis
entry.content: The KILLEKILL-run initiative supporting artists with disabilities has released a 19-track compilation, launched a crowdfunding campaign and announced a June fundraiser event.

entry.title: Cape Town listening bar One Park to close
entry.content: The Gardens venue will host a final run of events over the next few weeks.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Auntie Chanel
entry.content: A taste of Detroit's queer underground.

entry.title: Soft Cell announce final album Danceteria
entry.content: The 14-track LP is a tribute to the legendary New York club.

entry.title: Oklou, Fcukers, C418 among 2026 Libera Awards winners
entry.content: The 15th edition of the New York ceremony took place last night, celebrating the independent music industry.

entry.title: Listen to Thomas Bangalter's set on The Lot Radio
entry.content: The Daft Punk cofounder also confirmed there are no plans for a Daft Punk reunion.

entry.title: Space Afrika announce new album Quiet Storm
entry.content: The 12-track LP features Kelly Moran, Klein, Tony Njoku and Vanessa Bedoret.

entry.title: Soma Records unveils 35th anniversary remix EP series
entry.content: The Glasgow techno label has enlisted FJAAK, Mark Broom and .VRIL for the first instalment.

entry.title: Madrid venue Soundbaths to close
entry.content: Founder Le Nomad broke the news "with mixed emotions."

entry.title: Berlin's Hamburger Bahnhof to host Ryuichi Sakamoto exhibition
entry.content: seeing sound, hearing time will present seven works across five large-scale installations, opening during Berlin Art Week 2026.

entry.title: Sheffield's No Bounds Festival reveals 2026 lineup
entry.content: Mala, Max Cooper, KAVARI and gyrofield are among the bookings for the October weekender.

entry.title: New archival release from cult Scottish funk artist Jesse Rae announced
entry.content: The Flesher was originally composed as the soundtrack to Rae's feature-length film of the same name.

entry.title: What Would We Change About Modern Electronic Culture?
entry.content: Fewer sober raves? New music quota in DJ sets? Resident Advisor staff weigh in.

entry.title: Munich club BLITZ reveals June programme ahead of closure
entry.content: Âme, Mike Banks, Pancratio and Lara Fein will soundtrack the remainder of this month's events.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Maher Daniel
entry.content: A timeless festival set, recorded at Sunwaves in Bulgaria.

entry.title: underscores cancels September shows to join Charli xcx tour
entry.content: The New York artist confirmed that refunds will be issued and promised to "be back soon."

entry.title: Yoyaku announces new record shop in Barcelona
entry.content: The opening marks the company's first storefront outside of Paris.

entry.title: Watch Madonna's Confessions II film starring Honey Dijon, Arca
entry.content: The album promo video is streaming globally now via YouTube.

entry.title: Pitchfork Music Festival London announces first artists for 2026 edition
entry.content: Tortoise, ear, Olof Dreijer, keiyaA and Los Thuthanaka will play the multi-venue November event.

entry.title: Tresor Academy of Subcultural Understanding announces first global edition
entry.content: The programme will take place in Berlin from September 14th to October 4th.

entry.title: Floating Points reveals new album Mere Mortals
entry.content: The project was commissioned by the San Francisco Ballet.

entry.title: Berlin's Gaswerk Music Days finalises 2026 lineup
entry.content: New additions for the July and August event series include Kampire, Ecko Bazz and Pili Pili Girls.

entry.title: Christchurch club Hide to close after seven years
entry.content: "All great parties must come to an end," the team shared in a statement.

entry.title: Music union sues Universal and Warner over AI licensing revenues
entry.content: The American Federation of Musicians alleges major labels failed to compensate performers after settling copyright disputes with AI music firms Suno and Udio.

entry.title: London residents' group to oppose all new bar and restaurant licence applications
entry.content: The Soho Society's new policy prompted criticism from Mayor Sadiq Khan, who called the move "bad for London."

entry.title: RA.1042 Soft Crash
entry.content: Seven hours of camp, chug and genre-hopping hedonism from techno's two princes of darkness, recorded live at Kyiv's ∄.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Dreams
entry.content: Two hours of diverse electronic music from one of the year's best compilations.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: akii
entry.content: Expansive dubwise selections from the Tokyo-based DJ.

entry.title: This week's new music: FAUZIA, YHWH Nailgun, Tatyana Jane
entry.content: We round up ten new music announcements from the last few days.

entry.title: MOBOs founder Kanya King dies aged 57
entry.content: "The music world has lost one of its most fearless champions," awards organisers said in a statement.

entry.title: AEG Presents to take over Austin club Emo's
entry.content: The entertainment giant is set to rebrand the long-running venue. Emo's is planning to establish a new location in the city.

entry.title: Piezo lands on NAFF with new EP
entry.content: Sky Burial is out on June 25th.

entry.title: Jurango launches label, Staring At The Wall, shares three EPs
entry.content: Papa J The Great, FM Arlo and Aawadh are responsible for the label's first releases.

entry.title: This week's top stories: Primavera cancellations, new Skrillex album, Juno Download shuts
entry.content: Five key electronic music stories from the last few days.

entry.title: HÖR acquired by Berlin-based music services company 99Solutions
entry.content: 99Solutions managing director Stefanie Rohn described it as a "new chapter" for the troubled online broadcaster.

entry.title: Skrillex unveils surprise album SOMA
entry.content: The collab-heavy LP features Tracey, RHR, Blawan, Randomer and others.

entry.title: Primavera Sound cancels Massive Attack, Bad Gyal, Doja Cat amid Barcelona storms
entry.content: Severe weather and technical issues forced the cancellation of several major opening-night sets at the festival.

entry.title: Spekki Webu - Bootstrap Paradox
entry.content: Bending techno into thrilling new shapes, the Dutch artist conjures a trippy, alien album that speaks directly to rave culture's foundational spirit.

entry.title: Mix Of The Day: Genoe
entry.content: A patient, near-ten hour mix through an ever-shifting musical landscape.

entry.title: Sonic Liberation Devices to release first production instrument
entry.content: The Global South Drum Machine & Sampler is available to pre-order now.
